description: SHA-256 single round decomposition into Z80 u32 operations

round_function.formula: T1 = h + Σ1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K[i] + W[i]; T2 = Σ0(a) + Maj(a,b,c); h=g; g=f; f=e; e=d+T1; d=c; c=b; b=a; a=T1+T2

bitwise_ops.Ch(e,f,g): (e AND f) XOR (NOT e AND g)
bitwise_ops.Maj(a,b,c): (a AND b) XOR (a AND c) XOR (b AND c)

z80_implementation_notes.register_convention: u32 in DEHL (D=MSB). Second u32 via IX:IY, stack, or shadow EXX bank.

rotr_tricks.ROTR8: Free! Just rename bytes: DEHL→LHDE (byte rotation)
rotr_tricks.ROTR1: SRL D; RR E; RR H; RR L; JR NC,+2; SET 7,D (32-40T)
rotr_tricks.ROTR_N: Compose: ROTR8 + ROTR1×k, or ROTR16 + ROTR8 + ROTR1×k

rotr_decomposition.ROTR11: ROTR8 + ROTR1 × 3 = 0T + 96-120T = ~108T. Or: ROTR16 + ROTL1 × 5 = 4T + 170T. Former better.
rotr_decomposition.ROTR13: ROTR16 + ROTR1 × 3... wait, ROTR16=ROTR(16). 13 = 16-3 → ROTL3 after ROTR16? No, ROTR13 = ROTR16 + ROTL3 = EX DE,HL + 3×SHL32_wrap
rotr_decomposition.ROTR22: ROTR24 + ROTL2 = byte_rot_3 + 2×SHL32_wrap. byte_rot_3: DEHL→EDLH? Need careful byte shuffle.

z80_plan: Load e, f into DEHL, IX:IY
Load e, invert all 4 bytes: CPL on each via A (~32T)

Maj_abc.formula: (a AND b) XOR (a AND c) XOR (b AND c)
Maj_abc.optimization: Maj = (a AND b) OR (c AND (a XOR b)) — same op count but only 2 temps

round_total_estimate.notes: Previous estimate of 800T/round was optimistic. Real cost dominated by ROTR decomposition and lack of native 32-bit XOR/AND.
